warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity functions for furan were computed at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF, gas phase).  Using the supplied furan.xyz geometry (no re-optimization), single-point SCF calculations were run for the neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1), the cation (charge +1, doublet), and the anion (charge −1, doublet).  Mulliken charges from those three states yielded the condensed Fukui indices.  The most electrophilic and nucleophilic site is carbon 5 (index 4), whose f⁺ is 1.9242931 and f⁻ is 0.1616371; the dual descriptor confirms this site as the most reactive.

Method B3LYP/def2-TZVP DFT (no RI), PySCF 2.x, gas phase, charges via Mulliken.

All three SCF steps converged; the engine’s integrity check flags the expected ∑f⁺ = ∑f⁻ = 1.0 (warnings reproduced verbatim below).  Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true.

Key files: result JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp27aexbbl/live.json.  A live log was streamed during the run to the standard chemkit fukui_<timestamp>.out file (available in the working directory).

Warnings (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
